assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Response configures Saturday 2-6 AM window using --maintenance-window-start and --maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA"

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides example command for Q1 code freeze with 30-day period using --add-maintenance-exclusion flags

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Provides specific command for November audit freeze covering the entire month with appropriate exclusion flags

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Explicitly states "'No upgrades' exclusions are limited to 30 days maximum. For longer freezes, chain multiple exclusions but be aware this increases security debt."

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Response mentions manual upgrades are needed for certain scenarios but doesn't explicitly explain that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows while auto-upgrades respect them

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends Extended release channel and explains it provides 24-month support with no automatic minor upgrades for maximum control

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Commands include all required exclusion flags: --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Includes "Security Debt Warning: Extended exclusion periods can cause clusters to fall behind on patches" and mentions version skew risks
